title: Report shows different expenses in Classic vs New Expensify — a corporate card expense attached to the report is visible in Classic but missing in New (when required tags/coding are incomplete).

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel!
___

**Version Number:** 
**Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked)
**Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction
**If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:**
**Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):**
**Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856
**Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:**
**Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart
**Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773153785305069)

## Action Performed:
1. Open the same expense report in Classic Expensify
2. Locate the merchant (xyz) within the report
3. Observe two (xyz) expenses are present on the report: One cash expense, One corporate card (Chase) expense
4. Open the corporate card  expense in Classic and note it is missing required coding (e.g., Locations/Departments)
5. Open the same report in New Expensify
6. Locate the merchant (xyz)
7. Observe only one  expense appears (the cash expense). The corporate card expense that is visible/attached in Classic is not shown in NewDot.
## Expected Result:
New Expensify should display the same set of expenses attached to the report as Classic. If an expense is missing required tags (e.g., Location), it should still be visible on the report with validation errors/warnings, not disappear from the report view.
## Actual Result:
In New Expensify, the corporate card expense attached to the report (visible in Classic) does not appear on the report at all. Only the cash expense is shown in NewDot. This creates a mismatch between Classic and New report contents. They are getting an export error related to the missing tag, but they can't see that on New Expensify, they shouldn't have to go back to Classic to check things if they were migrated.
## Workaround:
Unknown
## Platforms:
Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced:
- [ ] Android: App
- [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome
- [ ] iOS: App
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome
- [x] Windows: Chrome
- [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari

## Screenshots/Videos

The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773153785305069)

[View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.32-3 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @abbagail **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773078140772399) ## Action Performed: **PreCondition:** Have ASR Have "re-bill expenses to clients: Default to Billable" (Classic) and Billable default: Billable" (ND) set. The setting hasn't been changed/updated in the last few months 1. Company card imports to the account 2. Expenses are automatically added to the report due to ASR 3. Submit Expenses ## Expected Result: All expenses added to the report and workspace should default to Billable ## Actual Result: Expenses are not updating to match workspace Rules. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773078140772399) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

View the report total and all line items 5. click More > Export To > CSV 6. see all expenses and line items ## Expected Result: Have each line item on the report match the total and show up. body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.34-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-8199/folder/13176909/42898311/1238923925?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 10 **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Preconditions: User is an admin of a workspace and has enabled Expensify Cards in more features. Workflows MUST be disabled in the workspace. User has set a fully verified VBA before. Admin has assigned a card in ECard settings. Steps: 1. Navigate to your workspace editor > More features > Integrate 2. Enable Accounting 3. Complete the Xero flow using the test credentials in 1password 4. Navigate to Accounting page again 5. Verify you see a Card reconciliation option row Result: Card reconciliation option not displayed after connecting Xero ## Expected Result: A Card reconciliation option row should be displayed on the Accounting page after successfully connecting Xero. ## Actual Result: The Card reconciliation option is not displayed on the Accounting page after completing the Xero integration. This issue is reproducible when connect to QBO, NetSuite and Sage Intacct. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cd317e5f-183c-4116-95d0-1cb0c547661c [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
